metadata.title: Abemaciclib with endocrine therapy – adjuvant
metadata.indication: Abemaciclib with endocrine therapy for eligible HR-positive, HER2-negative, node-positive early breast cancer at high risk of recurrence.
metadata.partial_assessment_note: Only entered values are assessed; omitted clinical domains remain explicitly unassessed.

clinical_governance.prescriptive_authority: Treatment plan must be initiated by a Consultant Medical Oncologist.
clinical_governance.dose_modification_note: Any dose modification should be discussed with a Consultant.
clinical_governance.disclaimer: Decision-support encoding of the current NCCP source. It does not replace the official regimen, clinical judgement, local policy, prescribing review, or pharmacy verification.

haematological_toxicity_grade.label: Haematological toxicity grade

options.label: Grade 0 — no toxicity
options.description: No clinical or laboratory evidence of toxicity.

options.label: Grade 1 — mild
options.description: Mild or asymptomatic; observation only; intervention generally not indicated.

options.label: Grade 2 — moderate
options.description: Moderate; minimal/local/non-invasive intervention indicated; limits instrumental ADL.

options.label: Grade 3 — severe
options.description: Severe or medically significant; admission may be indicated; limits self-care ADL.

haematological_toxicity_grade.assessment_guidance: Prefer actual blood-count values; use this only when a grade has already been established.

diarrhoea_grade.label: Diarrhoea grade

options.label: Grade 2 — 4–6 stools/day over baseline; limits instrumental ADL
Grade 3 — ≥7 stools/day over baseline, incontinence or admission indicated; limits self-care ADL

diarrhoea_grade.assessment_guidance: Document baseline frequency, stools above baseline, nocturnal stools, continence, intake, hydration, IV fluid need and admission.

alt_grade.label: ALT elevation grade
alt_grade.assessment_guidance: Calculate from actual result and local ULN; interpret with bilirubin and clinical context.

ast_grade.label: AST elevation grade
ast_grade.assessment_guidance: Calculate from actual result and local ULN; interpret with bilirubin and clinical context.

options.label: Mild/moderate impairment — no protocol dose adjustment
Severe impairment — limited evidence; specialist review

output_templates.proceed: Entered values meet an encoded treatment threshold for the assessed component(s).
output_templates.delay: Entered values trigger an encoded delay threshold. Reassess according to the NCCP protocol.
output_templates.dose_reduce: An encoded dose-modification threshold is triggered. Consultant review is required.
output_templates.consultant_review: The entered result lies in a phase-dependent or non-deterministic range. Confirm the treatment phase and review the official NCCP protocol.

rule_engine.conflict_policy: Return the most restrictive applicable action and display every triggered rule.
rule_engine.missing_data_policy: Evaluate only supplied values. Do not infer missing values and do not block a partial assessment.

rules.explanation: Before initiation, haemoglobin ≥8 g/dL is recommended.
Grade 3 or 4 haematological toxicity requires suspension until recovery to grade 2 or lower.
Persistent or recurrent grade 2, or grade 3–4 diarrhoea, requires interruption and protocol-directed management.
Grade 3 or higher transaminase toxicity requires interruption and dose-modification review.
Child–Pugh C requires reducing abemaciclib dosing frequency to once daily.
Severe renal impairment or dialysis requires specialist review because evidence is limited.

supportive_care.supportive_medications_label: Adjuvant oral therapy low-emetogenic-risk antiemetic script
premedication_summary: Routine scheduled antiemetic premedication is not usually required.
Provide rescue antiemetic where clinically indicated.
other_supportive_care: Proactive diarrhoea education and access to loperamide according to local policy.
Prompt contact at first loose stool and maintain hydration.

ctcae_standard.reference: CTCAE grading as cited by the NCCP source protocol
ctcae_standard.display_requirement: Every grade-based input includes toxicity-specific criteria and practical assessment guidance.
